יָתַר: The hand (Yod) marks a covenant (Tav) at the head (Resh) — the working deed sealed at the top, the remainder that juts over the edge, what is left over after the covenant is set.
עַמּוֹן: The eye (Ayin) sees the mighty waters (Mem) hooked (Vav) to the living fish (Nun) — the watching eye over the turbulent tribal waters, Ammon as the people who see and teem at the edge.
חֻקָּה: The fence (Chet) follows the sun on the horizon (Qoph), revealed through the window (He) — the boundary that tracks the sun's circuit and is seen at every opening, the statute carved into the wall.
יָרׇבְעָם: The hand (Yod) of the chief (Resh) inside the house (Beth) sees (Ayin) the mighty waters (Mem) — the working leader whose household eyes the multitude, Jeroboam who contended with the people.
תָּמִיד: The covenant mark (Tav) over the waters (Mem), worked by the hand (Yod) through the door (Dalet) — the sign planted in the deep, hand pushing through the threshold without ceasing, a perpetual motion.
מַרְאֶה: The waters (Mem) of the chief (Resh) ox-strength (Aleph) revealed through the window (He) — the mighty head's power made visible through the opening, a vision beheld at the window.
גָּאַל: The camel (Gimel) carries the ox-strength (Aleph) driven by the goad (Lamed) — the one who lifts and bears the primal burden, the kinsman-redeemer who shoulders the cost and brings the lost one home.
שָׁלַם: The consuming fire (Shin) directed by the goad (Lamed) into the waters (Mem) — the teeth of destruction guided toward the deep until the burning ceases, peace as the fire made whole and still.
רוּץ: The head (Resh) hooked (Vav) to the fish hook's desire (Tsade) — the chief connected to urgent longing, the body pulled forward by the hook of pursuit, running headlong.
בְּעַד: The house (Beth) seen by the eye (Ayin) through the door (Dalet) — the dwelling perceived through the threshold, what is within and behind and through, the passage from inside to out.
כֹּל: The open palm (Kaph) of the goad (Lamed) — the yielded hand directed outward toward everything, the encompassing reach that gathers all.
שָׁכַח: The consuming teeth (Shin) open the palm (Kaph) toward the fence (Chet) — the fire that releases the hand's grip behind the wall, what was held slipping into the hidden room, forgotten.
בָּמָה: The house (Beth) rises over the waters (Mem), revealed through the window (He) — the dwelling set above the mighty chaos, beheld from afar, the high place where the wave crests.
כָּפַר: The open palm (Kaph) covers the mouth (Pe) of the chief (Resh) — the yielding hand pressed over the speaking head, silencing the accusation, the covering that atones and hides the guilt.
מְעַט: The waters (Mem) seen by the eye (Ayin) coiled by the snake (Tet) — the mighty flood reduced to what the eye can spot in a serpent's coil, a small and contained thing.
רֹחַב: The head (Resh) beyond the fence (Chet) inside the house (Beth) — the chief looking past the wall into the wide dwelling, the breadth of space that opens when barriers fall.
רָעָב: The head (Resh) sees with the eye (Ayin) an empty house (Beth) — the chief who looks and finds the dwelling bare, hunger as the sight of nothing inside the home.
קִרְאָה: The sun on the horizon (Qoph) draws the chief (Resh) and ox-strength (Aleph) toward the window (He) — the horizon-call pulling the strong leader to the opening, the encounter that comes to meet you face to face.
אֱדֹם: The ox-strength (Aleph) through the door (Dalet) into the waters (Mem) — the strong one entering the threshold of the red chaotic deep, Edom as the ruddy earth-power striding into the flood.
יַעַן: The hand (Yod) of the eye (Ayin) upon the living fish (Nun) — the working sight fixed on moving life, heeding because the hand sees the reason swimming beneath.
עוֹר: The eye (Ayin) hooked (Vav) to the head (Resh) — the visible surface connected to the body's chief, the skin as the outer covering that the eye sees first.
יַחַד: The hand (Yod) inside the fence (Chet) at the door (Dalet) — the working deed enclosed within the same wall and entering the same threshold, together as one unit behind a single gate.
שְׁבִיעִי: The teeth (Shin) inside the house (Beth) worked by the hand (Yod), seen by the eye (Ayin), and worked by the hand (Yod) again — the consuming rest inside the dwelling, the seventh day when the eye sees the completed work.
שָׂבַע: The teeth (Shin) consuming inside the house (Beth) until the eye (Ayin) is satisfied — the fire of eating within the dwelling until the seeing is full, satiated at last.
מֵהֶם: The waters (Mem) revealed through the window (He) back to the waters (Mem) — from the deep, beheld, and returning to the deep, the origin flowing out and pointing back to its source.
שָׁרַת: The fire/teeth (Shin) of the chief (Resh) sealed by the covenant mark (Tav) — the consuming devotion of the head, signed and bound to serve, the minister who attends with burning loyalty.
עֵשָׂו: The eye (Ayin) consumed by fire (Shin) and hooked (Vav) — the one who sees and burns and is snared, Esau caught by appetite and tethered to the moment.
חֲמוֹר: The fence (Chet) holds the mighty waters (Mem) hooked (Vav) to the head (Resh) — the walled beast carrying the weight of chaos, connected to its master's head, the donkey bearing burdens behind the barrier.
אֹרֶךְ: The ox-strength (Aleph) at the head (Resh), bent by the open palm (Kaph) — the primal power stretching from the first to the yielding end, length as the distance the strong arm reaches.
מָלַט: The waters (Mem) goaded (Lamed) past the coiling snake (Tet) — the mighty flow driven around the serpent's grip, slipping smooth and free, the escape that slides away from danger.
יֶתֶר: The hand (Yod) of the covenant mark (Tav) at the head (Resh) — the working deed sealed at the top that hangs over the edge, the remnant and excess that overhangs the rest.
טָהֵר: The coiled snake (Tet) breathes through the window (He) toward the head (Resh) — the contained impurity exhaled through the opening and released to the chief, purified by the breath that passes through.
אֱלָהּ: The ox-strength (Aleph) goaded (Lamed) and revealed by breath (He) — the mighty one driven forward and made known through the window, God as the strong power that teaches and is beheld.
הָפַךְ: Behold (He) the mouth (Pe) bending the open palm (Kaph) — the revealed word that turns the yielding hand over, the overturning that flips everything the mouth has spoken.
רָעַע: The head (Resh) of the eye (Ayin) seeing the eye (Ayin) — the chief perception doubled back on itself, evil as sight reflecting sight, the shattering that comes from seeing only ruin.
שִׂמְחָה: The fire/teeth (Shin) over the waters (Mem) enclosed by the fence (Chet), revealed through the window (He) — the burning delight that rises from the deep, contained and then shown forth, joy bursting through the opening.
דַּעַת: The door (Dalet) of the eye (Ayin) sealed by the covenant mark (Tav) — the threshold of perception marked and established, knowledge as the signed doorway through which seeing passes.
טָהוֹר: The coiled snake (Tet) breathes (He), hooked (Vav) to the head (Resh) — what was wrapped in impurity exhales through the window and connects to the chief, pure as the venom drawn out and the head made clean.
פֶּשַׁע: The mouth (Pe) consumed by fire (Shin) before the eye (Ayin) — the speaking edge devoured by its own flame in full view, revolt as the word that burns and is seen by all.
אַחְאָב: The ox-strength (Aleph) fenced (Chet) from the ox-strength (Aleph) inside the house (Beth) — the strong one walled off from the first power within the dwelling, Ahab as the brother of the father divided from strength.
זָנָה: The weapon (Zayin) cuts the living fish (Nun) open to the window (He) — the blade that severs life and exposes it, adultery as the cutting of the living bond laid bare for all to see.
כֶּרֶם: The open palm (Kaph) of the chief (Resh) over the waters (Mem) — the yielding hand of the head tending the mighty flow, the vineyard as the cupped hand that catches the rain for the vines.
גֵּר: The camel (Gimel) before the chief (Resh) — the wandering beast arriving at the head of the household, the stranger who carries his life on his back and stands before the master.
קָצֶה: The sun on the horizon (Qoph) hooks the desire (Tsade), revealed at the window (He) — the farthest point where the horizon's line catches the last longing, the extremity beheld at the edge of sight.
עֹז: The eye (Ayin) of the weapon (Zayin) — the perception behind the blade, the seeing that wields the cutting edge, strength as the force that both perceives and strikes.
חֶלֶב: The inner room (Chet) goads (Lamed) toward the house (Beth) — what is directed from the hidden interior into the dwelling, the richest part stored deep inside, the fat reserved within.
זְרוֹעַ: The weapon (Zayin) at the head (Resh) hooked (Vav) to the eye (Ayin) — the blade of the chief connected to the seeing, the arm as the striking limb that extends power and sight outward.
שִׁבְעִים: The teeth (Shin) inside the house (Beth) seen by the eye (Ayin), worked by the hand (Yod) in the waters (Mem) — the consuming fullness within the dwelling, watched and worked through the mighty deep, seventy as the completed cycle of seven multiplied.
סָגַר: The prop/support (Samech) lifts the camel (Gimel) before the chief (Resh) — the pillar that carries the burden and blocks the head's way, shutting the entrance where the laden beast stands firm.
תָּמִים: The covenant mark (Tav) over the waters (Mem) worked by the hand (Yod) through waters (Mem) again — the sign set in the deep, labored through, and returning to the deep unblemished, entire and without spot.
כְּרוּב: The open palm (Kaph) of the chief (Resh) hooked (Vav) inside the house (Beth) — the yielding hand of the head connected within the dwelling, the cherub as the bowing guardian stationed inside the holy place.
מַלְכוּת: The waters (Mem) goaded (Lamed) by the open palm (Kaph), hooked (Vav) to the covenant mark (Tav) — the mighty flow directed by the bending hand and fastened to the sign, kingdom as the ruled dominion sealed by covenant.
חָרָה: The fence (Chet) of the chief (Resh) revealed at the window (He) — the inner wall of the head burning and showing through, anger as the hidden fire that glows and is seen.
שָׁמַד: The consuming teeth (Shin) of the waters (Mem) crash through the door (Dalet) — the fire-flood that devours the threshold, desolation as the force that destroys every entrance.
כְּנַעַן: The open palm (Kaph) of the living fish (Nun) seen by the eye (Ayin) and the fish (Nun) again — the yielding hand of the swarming life perceived and teeming, Canaan as the land of traders whose busy hands never rest.
עֵבֶר: The eye (Ayin) inside the house (Beth) of the chief (Resh) — the sight passing through the dwelling of the head, the other side as what the eye sees when it crosses the household threshold.
חָלָל: The fence (Chet) pierced by the goad (Lamed) and the goad (Lamed) again — the wall driven through twice, the double puncture that breaches the inner room, the slain one pierced and profaned.
יֶלֶד: The hand (Yod) goaded (Lamed) through the door (Dalet) — the deed directed toward the threshold and pushed through, the child as what is brought forth through the opening into the world.
עֵצָה: The eye (Ayin) hooks the desire (Tsade), revealed through the window (He) — the perception that catches what is sought and shows it at the opening, counsel as the seeing that draws wisdom into view.
שֶׁקֶל: The consuming teeth (Shin) follow the sun (Qoph) to the goad (Lamed) — the fire that tracks the horizon and is directed to the measure, the shekel as the weight that the consuming market drives toward the standard.
עָנָן: The eye (Ayin) of the living fish (Nun) and the fish (Nun) again — the seeing of life upon life, the cloud as the teeming moisture that the eye perceives swimming above, layer upon layer.
אֱמֹרִי: The ox-strength (Aleph) in the waters (Mem) at the head (Resh) worked by the hand (Yod) — the mighty one rising from the deep to the summit, the Amorite as the highland dweller whose strength commands the peaks.
שֶׁלֶם: The consuming fire (Shin) goaded (Lamed) into the waters (Mem) — the teeth directed to the deep until the burning is quenched, the peace offering as the sacrifice that makes all whole.
שִׁיר: The teeth (Shin) worked by the hand (Yod) at the head (Resh) — the consuming fire shaped by the working hand into the chief melody, the song as fire made into music by skillful hands.
לָמַד: The goad (Lamed) drives the waters (Mem) through the door (Dalet) — the ox goad that pushes the mighty flow through the threshold, teaching as the directed force that moves knowledge through the opening.
דֶּלֶת: The door (Dalet) goaded (Lamed) to the covenant mark (Tav) — the threshold directed toward the sign, the swinging door that moves on its hinge toward the mark and back again.
פֵּאָה: The mouth (Pe) of the ox-strength (Aleph) breathing (He) — the speaking edge of the first power revealed, the corner as the boundary where the strong voice echoes from the side.
שָׁמֵם: The consuming teeth (Shin) of the waters (Mem) and the waters (Mem) again — fire crashing through wave after wave, the double desolation of flood and flame, the stunned emptiness left behind.
בָּעַר: The house (Beth) where the eye (Ayin) sees the chief (Resh) — the dwelling where perception meets the head and ignites, the kindling that blazes when the seeing eye finds fuel within the home.
רָחוֹק: The head (Resh) behind the fence (Chet), hooked (Vav) to the sun on the horizon (Qoph) — the chief separated by the wall and connected only to the farthest horizon, remote as the distance between the walled self and the setting sun.
קָנָה: The sun on the horizon (Qoph) draws the living fish (Nun) to the window (He) — the horizon pulling life toward the opening, to acquire as the act of reaching for what rises and making it one's own.
פָּלַל: The mouth (Pe) goaded (Lamed) and goaded (Lamed) again — the speaking directed and redirected, prayer as the doubled turning of the word toward its target, judging and interceding in the same breath.
יְהוֹשָׁפָט: The hand (Yod) reveals (He) and hooks (Vav) the consuming fire (Shin) to the mouth (Pe) surrounded by the snake (Tet) — the working hand of the revealed one connecting the fire to the spoken judgment, Jehoshaphat whose God judges.
שִׁית: The consuming fire (Shin) worked by the hand (Yod) sealed by the covenant mark (Tav) — the teeth set by the working hand upon the mark, to place as the deliberate act of setting something where it is signed to be.
בַּעַל: The house (Beth) where the eye (Ayin) wields the goad (Lamed) — the dwelling in which the seeing one directs and drives, the master who owns and commands from within his household.
פֹּה: The mouth (Pe) breathing (He) — the speaking that exhales and reveals, here as the place where the word is breathed, this very spot where the mouth opens.
קָלַל: The sun on the horizon (Qoph) goads (Lamed) and goads again (Lamed) — the horizon driving twice with its sharp point, the thing made light and swift by double goading, the curse that cuts both ways.
הָמוֹן: Behold (He) the waters (Mem) hooked (Vav) to the living fish (Nun) — the revealed deep connected to swarming life, the roaring noise of the multitude churning like a sea full of fish.
לָא: The goad (Lamed) turned back from the ox (Aleph) — the directing stick that finds no strength to drive, the negation where the goad meets nothing, not, no, without.
סָתַר: The prop/support (Samech) sealed by the covenant mark (Tav) at the head (Resh) — the pillar covered with the sign before the chief, hiding as the act of surrounding the marked thing and concealing it from the leader's sight.
עָזַר: The eye (Ayin) of the weapon (Zayin) at the chief (Resh) — the seeing blade that surrounds the head and guards it, help as the watchful sword-arm that circles the leader.
מָשַׁל: The waters (Mem) consumed by fire (Shin) and directed by the goad (Lamed) — the mighty deep devoured and driven, to rule as the power that both consumes the chaos and directs its course.
גּוּר: The camel (Gimel) hooked (Vav) to the chief (Resh) — the wandering beast tethered to the head of the household, the sojourner who dwells in awe at the tent peg of the master.
עוּר: The eye (Ayin) hooked (Vav) to the head (Resh) — the seeing connected to the chief who was sleeping, the eye pulling upward to wake the head, to rouse and stir from slumber.
הֵיכָל: Behold (He) the hand (Yod) of the open palm (Kaph) directed by the goad (Lamed) — the revealed work of the yielding hand driven toward its purpose, the palace as the great hall where every hand bows and every goad points to the throne.
אֲשֶׁר: The ox-strength (Aleph) consumed by fire (Shin) at the head (Resh) — the primal power that burns its way to the chief, the one who is singled out and identified.
בֵּן: The house (Beth) of the living fish (Nun) — life continuing inside the family dwelling, the one who carries the household forward, a son.
כִּי: The open palm (Kaph) working (Yod) — the hand that yields and acts, the gesture of acknowledgment that makes something certain, for, because.
עָשָׂה: The eye (Ayin) perceives, the teeth (Shin) consume, and the breath (He) reveals — to see what must be done, devour the raw material, and bring forth the finished work.
יִשְׂרָאֵל: The hand (Yod) consumes by fire (Shin) at the head (Resh) of the ox-strength (Aleph) goaded (Lamed) — the working hand that wrestles, prevails at the top, and directs the mighty one, Israel.
פָּנִים: The mouth (Pe) of the living fish (Nun) worked by hand (Yod) over the waters (Mem) — the speaking, breathing surface turned outward in active life, the face as presence shown to the world.
בַּיִת: The house (Beth) worked by the hand (Yod), sealed with the covenant mark (Tav) — a dwelling shaped by labor and bound by promise, a household built and consecrated.
דָּבָר: The door (Dalet) of the house (Beth) opened by the chief (Resh) — what passes through the threshold of the dwelling from the head, the spoken word that enters and changes everything.
שָׁמַע: The teeth (Shin) devouring the waters (Mem) perceived by the eye (Ayin) — the consuming flood made visible, hearing as the moment sound crashes into awareness.
יָשַׁב: The hand (Yod) consumed by fire (Shin) inside the house (Beth) — the working hand that settles into the dwelling and stays, to sit, to remain, to inhabit.
דָּוִד: The door (Dalet) hooked (Vav) to the door (Dalet) — two doorways connected, the beloved one who stands as a passage between heaven and earth, David.
שָׁנֶה: The teeth (Shin) of the living fish (Nun) revealed by breath (He) — the consuming cycle of life made visible again, one year devouring the next and showing itself anew.
שֵׁם: The teeth (Shin) over the waters (Mem) — fire hovering above the deep, the distant burning place, there, in that place beyond.
שָׁלַח: The teeth (Shin) goaded (Lamed) through the fence (Chet) — fire driven past the barrier, the consuming force directed beyond the boundary, to send forth.
הִנֵּה: Behold (He), the living fish (Nun) revealed by breath (He) — life bracketed by two windows of revelation, the sudden gasp of seeing, lo! behold!
שָׁם: The teeth (Shin) over the waters (Mem) — fire hovering above the deep, the distant burning place, there, in that place beyond.
אִשָּׁה: The ox-strength (Aleph) consumed by fire (Shin) and revealed by breath (He) — primal strength refined through flame and breathed into being, woman as the living fire of the household.
גַּם: The camel (Gimel) crossing the waters (Mem) — the beast of burden that carries through chaos, also, even, the one who adds and gathers together.
שְׁנַיִם: The teeth (Shin) of the living fish (Nun) worked by hand (Yod) in the waters (Mem) — consuming life laboring in the deep, two as the doubled reflection in the flood.
מֹשֶׁה: The waters (Mem) consumed by fire (Shin) and revealed by breath (He) — the one drawn from chaos through flame into revelation, Moses, pulled from the water.
כֹּהֵן: The open palm (Kaph) revealed through the window (He) of the living fish (Nun) — the yielded hand breathing life into view, the priest who stands between and mediates with open hands.
אֵלֶּה: The ox-strength (Aleph) goaded (Lamed) and revealed by breath (He) — the mighty ones driven forward into sight, these, the ones pointed out and made visible.
אַתָּה: The ox-strength (Aleph) sealed by covenant (Tav) and revealed by breath (He) — the strong one marked and made visible, thou, you who stand before me identified.
כֵּן: The open palm (Kaph) of the living fish (Nun) — the yielding hand that holds life steady, set upright, established, so, thus.
דֶּרֶךְ: The door (Dalet) where the chief (Resh) bends the palm (Kaph) — the threshold the leader passes through with yielded hand, the road, the way one walks.
נָשָׂא: The living fish (Nun) consumed by fire (Shin) back to the ox-strength (Aleph) — swimming life devoured and raised to primal power, to lift, to carry, to bear up.
יְרוּשָׁלִַ͏ם: The hand (Yod) of the chief (Resh) hooked (Vav) through fire (Shin), goaded (Lamed) over the waters (Mem) — the working head connected through consuming flame, directed over the deep, Jerusalem, the foundation of peace.
בַּת: The house (Beth) sealed by the covenant mark (Tav) — the dwelling bound by promise, a daughter as the signed and treasured one of the family.
כֹּה: The open palm (Kaph) revealed by breath (He) — the yielding hand made visible, like this, in this manner, the gesture that shows how.
גָּדוֹל: The camel (Gimel) passing through the door (Dalet), hooked (Vav) to the goad (Lamed) — the great beast carrying its load through the threshold, driven onward, great, mighty.
תַּחַת: The covenant mark (Tav) enclosing the fence (Chet) sealed again by the mark (Tav) — a sign bounding the inner room on both sides, beneath, under, in place of.
פֶּה: The mouth (Pe) revealed by breath (He) — speech made visible through the window, the mouth as the opening where words become air.
שָׁמַר: The teeth (Shin) over the waters (Mem) watched by the chief (Resh) — consuming fire above the chaos guarded by the head, to keep, to watch, to hedge about and protect.
עַתָּה: The eye (Ayin) sees the covenant mark (Tav) revealed by breath (He) — perception fixed on the sign made visible, now, at this very moment, the present seen and seized.
שָׁלוֹשׁ: The teeth (Shin) goaded (Lamed) and hooked (Vav) back to the teeth (Shin) — fire directed and connected returning to fire, three as the cord braided back upon itself.
מִשְׁפָּט: The waters (Mem) consumed by fire (Shin), spoken (Pe) and surrounded by the snake (Tet) — the flood refined through flame, declared aloud, and contained, a verdict, a judgment rendered.
שָׁמַיִם: The teeth (Shin) consuming the waters (Mem), worked by hand (Yod) into waters (Mem) — fire pressed into water pressed into fire, the burning expanse of heaven.
שַׂר: The teeth (Shin) of the chief (Resh) — consuming fire at the head, the one who devours obstacles from the top, a ruler, a prince, a captain.
תָּוֶךְ: The covenant mark (Tav) hooked (Vav) to the open palm (Kaph) — the sign connected to the yielding hand, what is bound and held in the middle, the midst, the center.
בֵּין: The house (Beth) with the working hand (Yod) of the living fish (Nun) — the active living space between two things, the dwelling place that separates one from another, between.
שָׁאוּל: The teeth (Shin) of ox-strength (Aleph) hooked (Vav) to the goad (Lamed) — consuming power connected and driven forward, Saul, the one asked for and demanded.
כֶּסֶף: The open palm (Kaph) propped (Samech) by the mouth (Pe) — the yielded hand supported by speech, silver as what is spoken for and desired, the currency of longing.
מִזְבֵּחַ: The waters (Mem) of the weapon (Zayin) inside the house (Beth) enclosed by the fence (Chet) — the mighty cutting place within the bounded dwelling, an altar where sacrifice is slain.
שֶׁבַע: The teeth (Shin) inside the house (Beth) seen by the eye (Ayin) — consuming completeness within the dwelling perceived, seven, the full number witnessed.
בָּנָה: The house (Beth) of the living fish (Nun) revealed by breath (He) — life within the dwelling made visible, to build, to raise up a household into being.
שַׁעַר: The teeth (Shin) seen by the eye (Ayin) of the chief (Resh) — the consuming threshold perceived by the head, a gate, the opening where the ruler watches.
דָּם: The door (Dalet) of the waters (Mem) — the threshold of chaos, blood as the red flood that pours through the opening between life and death.
דִּי: The door (Dalet) of the hand (Yod) — the threshold of action, that which, enough, the point where the deed meets its limit.
עָשָׂר: The eye (Ayin) sees the fire (Shin) of the chief (Resh) — perception of the consuming head, ten, the full count visible at the top.
בָּרַךְ: The house (Beth) of the chief (Resh) yielding the palm (Kaph) — the head of the household bending the knee with open hand, to bless, to kneel in the presence of greatness.
כְּלִי: The open palm (Kaph) goaded (Lamed) by the hand (Yod) — the yielded hand directed and worked into purpose, a vessel, an instrument, a thing prepared for use.
שָׂדֶה: The fire (Shin) at the door (Dalet) revealed by breath (He) — burning heat opening through the threshold into the visible expanse, a field, the open land under the sun.
אַרְבַּע: The ox-strength (Aleph) of the chief (Resh) inside the house (Beth) seen by the eye (Ayin) — primal power at the head of the dwelling perceived from all sides, four, the number of corners.
עֶשְׂרִים: The eye (Ayin) sees fire (Shin) at the chief (Resh), worked by hand (Yod) in the waters (Mem) — perception of the consuming head laboring through the deep, twenty, a doubled ten.
מִשְׁפָּחָה: The waters (Mem) consumed by fire (Shin), spoken (Pe) within the fence (Chet), revealed by breath (He) — the mighty flood refined and declared within the bounded space made visible, a family, a clan.
פָּקַד: The mouth (Pe) follows the sun (Qoph) through the door (Dalet) — speech chasing the horizon to the threshold, to visit, to appoint, to call to account at the entry.
שְׁלֹמֹה: The teeth (Shin) goaded (Lamed) over the waters (Mem), revealed by breath (He) — fire directed across the chaos and made peaceful, Solomon, the one whose consuming power brings wholeness.
חַטָּאָה: The fence (Chet) containing the snake (Tet) back to ox-strength (Aleph), revealed by breath (He) — the inner room where the serpent coils around primal power and is exposed, sin, the offense made visible.
כָּרַת: The open palm (Kaph) of the chief (Resh) sealed by covenant (Tav) — the yielding hand of the head making the mark, to cut, to cut a covenant, to sever and bind.
פְּלִשְׁתִּי: The mouth (Pe) goaded (Lamed) through fire (Shin), sealed by covenant (Tav) and worked by hand (Yod) — speech driven through the consuming flame, marked and labored, Philistine, the rolling invader.
לֵוִיִּי: The goad (Lamed) hooked (Vav) to the working hand (Yod) and hand (Yod) again — direction connected to doubled labor, Levite, the one joined and attached to service.
בְּרִית: The house (Beth) of the chief (Resh) worked by hand (Yod) and sealed with the mark (Tav) — what the head builds by labor and signs, a covenant, a compact cut and bound.
מִדְבָּר: The waters (Mem) at the door (Dalet) of the house (Beth) under the chief (Resh) — chaos at the threshold of the dwelling ruled from above, the wilderness, the place of open speech where God leads.
בָּשָׂר: The house (Beth) consumed by fire (Shin) at the chief (Resh) — the dwelling devoured from the top, flesh as the mortal covering that wastes and is consumed.
פַּרְעֹה: The mouth (Pe) of the chief (Resh) seen by the eye (Ayin) and revealed by breath (He) — the speaking head perceived and beheld, Pharaoh, the great house whose word is seen.
בָּבֶל: The house (Beth) inside the house (Beth) goaded (Lamed) — dwelling within dwelling directed outward, Babel, the confusion of building upon building driven by ambition.
רָשָׁע: The chief (Resh) consumed by fire (Shin) and seen by the eye (Ayin) — the head devoured by its own flame and exposed, the wicked one whose wrong is visible.
מַטֶּה: The waters (Mem) surrounding the snake (Tet), revealed by breath (He) — the mighty coil made visible, a rod, a staff, a branch that extends and rules like a tribe.
אַמָּה: The ox-strength (Aleph) of the waters (Mem), revealed by breath (He) — primal power of the deep made visible, a mother, the mighty source from which life is measured.
שָׁבַר: The teeth (Shin) inside the house (Beth) of the chief (Resh) — fire consuming the dwelling from the head down, to break, to shatter, to burst apart from within.
שָׁחַת: The teeth (Shin) within the fence (Chet) sealed by the mark (Tav) — fire consuming the inner room and branding it, to decay, to destroy, to corrupt what was enclosed.
שָׂנֵא: The teeth (Shin) of the living fish (Nun) turned against the ox-strength (Aleph) — consuming life devouring the strong one, hatred as the living flame that attacks the leader.
מְנַשֶּׁה: The waters (Mem) of the living fish (Nun) consumed by fire (Shin) and revealed by breath (He) — the deep life forgotten through burning and then seen, Manasseh, causing to forget.
כְּמוֹ: The open palm (Kaph) over the waters (Mem) hooked (Vav) — the yielding hand connected above the chaos, as, like, the comparison that links one thing to another.
שְׁמוּאֵל: The teeth (Shin) of the waters (Mem) hooked (Vav) to ox-strength (Aleph) goaded (Lamed) — the consuming deep connected to primal power and driven forward, Samuel, heard of God.
נְחֹשֶׁת: The living fish (Nun) within the fence (Chet) consumed by fire (Shin) and sealed by covenant (Tav) — life enclosed and refined by flame and marked, copper, bronze forged in the furnace.
מִשְׁכָּן: The waters (Mem) consumed by fire (Shin), yielded by the palm (Kaph) of the living fish (Nun) — the mighty flame given over by the hand of life, a tabernacle, a dwelling place where presence rests.
פָּנָה: The mouth (Pe) of the living fish (Nun) revealed by breath (He) — speech turning with life toward the visible, to turn, to face, to look in a new direction.
כִּסֵּא: The open palm (Kaph) propped (Samech) by ox-strength (Aleph) — the yielding hand supported by primal power, a throne, a seat upheld by the might of the ruler.
פָּתַח: The mouth (Pe) of the covenant mark (Tav) breaking through the fence (Chet) — the word of promise piercing the barrier, to open wide, to set free from the enclosure.
אַרְבָּעִים: The ox-strength (Aleph) of the chief (Resh) inside the house (Beth), seen by the eye (Ayin), worked by the hand (Yod) in the waters (Mem) — primal power at the head of the dwelling tested through labor in the deep, forty.
מִסְפָּר: The waters (Mem) propped (Samech) by the mouth (Pe) of the chief (Resh) — the chaos supported by the word of the head, a number, an account spoken and upheld.
גִּלְעָד: The camel (Gimel) goaded (Lamed), seen by the eye (Ayin) at the door (Dalet) — the lifting beast driven into view at the threshold, Gilead, the heap of witness at the border.
שֶׁמֶשׁ: The teeth (Shin) of the waters (Mem) consumed by teeth (Shin) — fire devouring the deep and fire again, the sun as the doubled flame that burns above and below the horizon.
פֵּן: The mouth (Pe) of the living fish (Nun) — the speaking life that warns, lest, perhaps, the word of caution before the creature swims into danger.
פַּר: The mouth (Pe) of the chief (Resh) — the bellowing head, a young bull, the strong one whose voice comes from the front.
שָׁאַר: The teeth (Shin) of ox-strength (Aleph) at the chief (Resh) — fire that consumed all but the strongest at the head, to remain, the remnant that survives the burning.
חִזְקִיָּה: The fence (Chet) armed with a weapon (Zayin), following the sun (Qoph), worked by the hand (Yod) and revealed by breath (He) — the enclosed strength that cuts its way to the horizon through labor, Hezekiah, strengthened by God.
נָשִׂיא: The living fish (Nun) consumed by fire (Shin), worked by hand (Yod) back to ox-strength (Aleph) — life refined through flame and labor into primal power, an exalted one, a prince who rises.
שָׁכַן: The teeth (Shin) yielded by the palm (Kaph) of the living fish (Nun) — fire given over by the hand of life, to dwell, to settle, to let the consuming flame rest and reside.
כֹּחַ: The open palm (Kaph) within the fence (Chet) — the hand contained inside the enclosure, vigor as the coiled strength held within the bounded space, power ready to strike.
שָׁלַךְ: The teeth (Shin) goaded (Lamed) by the open palm (Kaph) — fire driven outward by the yielding hand, to throw, to cast away, to hurl with a sweeping motion.
חָשַׁב: The fence (Chet) consumed by fire (Shin) inside the house (Beth) — the inner room burning within the dwelling, to think, to weave, to devise plans inside the mind's chamber.
בָּטַח: The house (Beth) surrounded by the snake (Tet) within the fence (Chet) — the dwelling enclosed and guarded by coiling walls, to trust, to feel secure inside double protection.
פְּרִי: The mouth (Pe) of the chief (Resh) worked by the hand (Yod) — what the leader's speech produces through labor, fruit as the harvest of word and deed.
יָשָׁר: The hand (Yod) consuming (Shin) toward the chief (Resh) — the working deed pressing through fire to the head, straight, upright, the direct path to the top.
בְּכוֹר: The house (Beth) of the open palm (Kaph) hooked (Vav) to the chief (Resh) — the dwelling's yielding hand connected to the head, the firstborn, the one who opens the family and links to the father.
פַּעַם: The mouth (Pe) seen by the eye (Ayin) in the waters (Mem) — speech perceived in the deep, a beat, a step, a stroke, the recurring pulse like a foot striking water.
שָׂרַף: The teeth (Shin) of the chief (Resh) speaking (Pe) — fire at the head crying out, to burn, the consuming blaze that roars from the source.
בָּכָה: The house (Beth) of the open palm (Kaph) revealed by breath (He) — the dwelling where the yielded hand lifts to the window, to weep, the cry that opens from within the home.
שָׁפַךְ: The teeth (Shin) speaking (Pe) through the open palm (Kaph) — fire pouring from the mouth through the yielding hand, to spill forth, to pour out like blood or water.
כָּבַד: The open palm (Kaph) inside the house (Beth) at the door (Dalet) — the heavy hand weighing down the dwelling at the threshold, to be heavy, to be glorious, the gravity of honor.
גָּדַל: The camel (Gimel) at the door (Dalet) driven by the goad (Lamed) — the great beast pushing through the threshold directed forward, to grow, to become great, to magnify.
שֶׁקֶר: The teeth (Shin) following the sun (Qoph) to the chief (Resh) — fire chasing the horizon toward the head but never reaching it, a lie, untruth, the deception that pursues a false light.
בִּלְתִּי: The house (Beth) goaded (Lamed) toward the covenant mark (Tav) worked by hand (Yod) — the dwelling driven to the sign but falling short by labor, without, except, the failure that negates.
שַׁבָּת: The teeth (Shin) resting in the house (Beth), sealed by the covenant mark (Tav) — the consuming fire stilled within the dwelling and signed, Sabbath, the intermission sealed by promise.
אֲבִישָׁלוֹם: The ox-strength (Aleph) of the house (Beth), worked by hand (Yod), consumed by fire (Shin), goaded (Lamed) and hooked (Vav) over the waters (Mem) — the father's mighty dwelling laboring through flame toward the connected deep, Absalom, father of peace.
כָּנָף: The open palm (Kaph) of the living fish (Nun) speaking (Pe) — the yielding hand of life spreading outward with breath, a wing, the feathered edge that catches the air and lifts.
